anti_patterns.name: Anemic Domain Model
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/ -- most domain types (Buffer, Cursor, Viewport) are data containers; all behavior lives in free functions in the same module rather than on the types themselves

anti_patterns.name: Leaking Tactical Patterns into TUI Shell
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs -- InputState enum, Operator enum, execute_motion, execute_delete_with_motion are domain logic living in the imperative shell rather than alfred-core

anti_patterns.name: Missing Domain Events
anti_patterns.location: No domain event types defined; hook system (alfred-core/hook.rs) provides dispatching infrastructure but hooks are stringly-typed with no domain event model

anti_patterns.name: Shared Kernel Overload
anti_patterns.location: crates/alfred-core/src/ contains 12 modules covering unrelated concerns (buffer, cursor, rainbow_csv, theme, text_object, viewport, panel, hook, command, key_event, error, editor_state)

context_map_mermaid:
graph TD
  Core["alfred-core\n(Pure Domain Kernel)\nBuffer, Cursor, Viewport,\nCommands, Keymaps, Hooks,\nTheme, Panels, EditorState"]
  Lisp["alfred-lisp\n(FFI Bridge)\nLispRuntime, Bridge primitives"]
  Plugin["alfred-plugin\n(Plugin Lifecycle)\nDiscovery, Registry, Metadata"]
  Syntax["alfred-syntax\n(Syntax Highlighting)\nSyntaxHighlighter, LanguageConfig"]
  TUI["alfred-tui\n(Imperative Shell)\nEvent loop, Renderer, InputState"]
  Bin["alfred-bin\n(Composition Root)\nmain, run_editor, load_plugins"]
  Plugins["plugins/\n(Lisp Extension Layer)\nvim-keybindings, status-bar,\nline-numbers, browse-mode, etc."]

  Core -->|"depended on by"| Lisp
  Core -->|"depended on by"| Plugin
  Core -->|"depended on by"| Syntax
  Core -->|"depended on by"| TUI
  Lisp -->|"depended on by"| Plugin
  Lisp -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Plugin -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  TUI -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Syntax -->|"depended on by"| Bin
  Bin -->|"loads at runtime"| Plugins
  Plugins -->|"calls via bridge"| Lisp
